Propulsion Fluids Chaos
Research Experience Publications

Reacting flow, instability, and nonlinear dynamics

Yue Weng

I study how flames, flows, and complex dynamical systems organize, transition, and become unstable. I am currently a Postdoctoral Associate at the University of Minnesota and will join Mississippi State University as an Assistant Professor in August 2026.

View publications Current role
Now Postdoctoral Associate

Department of Mechanical Engineering

University of Minnesota Twin Cities

Next Assistant Professor

Department of Aerospace Engineering

Mississippi State University, August 2026

Research Interests

How coherent behavior emerges in fluid and flame systems

Combustion instability

Thermoacoustic transitions, flame dynamics, synchronization, and instability mechanisms in reacting flows.

Complex systems

Nonlinear dynamics, bifurcations, coherent structures, and reduced descriptions of high-dimensional flows.

Flow imaging

Advanced diagnostics including digital holography, 3D particle tracking velocimetry, and data-rich experiments.

Publications

Selected Journal Articles

  1. Weng, Y., Zhu, Y., Saha, A. (2025). Vortex-driven subharmonic bifurcation in a multi-flame Rijke tube. Combustion and Flame, 282, 114447. DOI
  2. Weng, Y., Potnis, A., Unni, V. R., Saha, A. (2024). Assessing local statistics of a premixed turbulent Bunsen flame. AIAA Journal, 1-9. DOI
  3. Weng, Y., Unni, V. R., Sujith, R. I., Saha, A. (2023). Synchronization-based model for turbulent thermoacoustic systems. Nonlinear Dynamics, 111, 12113-12126. DOI
  4. Weng, Y., Potnis, A., Saha, A. (2023). Regime and morphology of polyhedral Bunsen flames. Combustion and Flame, 248, 112585. DOI
  5. Weng, Y., Unni, V. R., Sujith, R. I., Saha, A. (2020). Synchronization framework for modeling transition to thermoacoustic instability in laminar combustors. Nonlinear Dynamics, 100, 3295-3306. DOI
